title: Game State, Scenes, Pause, Life Loss, Restart, and Ownership · Behavior Case: Transition Contract
summary: Menus, play, pause, death, and game over are booleans that form impossible combinations; pause leaves timers running; restart retains old projectiles and score. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the transition contract increment in explicit scene and run-lifecycle state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-…
objectives: Define trigger, guard, exit work, entry work, resulting state, and rejected transitions.
Represent boot, menu, playing, paused, life-lost, game-over, settings, and exit as explicit valid states.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Predict Transition Contract · Theory Transition Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Asteroids team handles case 8090 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to model menu, playing, paused, life-lost, game-over, and restart transitions without hidden scene state; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-game-state-scenes-5
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-game-state-scenes-5.
# Case 9036: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def worked_asteroids_game_state_scenes_5(states: int = 6, valid_transitions: int = 9, invalid_transitions: int = 0, pause_freezes_simulation: bool = True, restart_resets_run: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "playerworked5",
        "seed9036",
        "input5dekrow",
        "frameworked59036",
        "state9036worked5",
        "playtest5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if states < 4 or valid_transitions < states:
        return False, states, "state-graph"
    if invalid_transitions > 0:
        return False, invalid_transitions, "illegal-transition"
    if not pause_freezes_simulation:
        return False, valid_transitions, "pause-leak"
    if not restart_resets_run:
        return False, valid_transitions, "restart-leak"
    assert valid_transitions >= states
    return True, valid_transitions, "explicit-scenes"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python Asteroids decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected frame, input, seam, collision, cue, save, package, or release case, then state which native pygame-ce, SDL, display, controller, audio, filesystem, GPU, accessibility playtest, installer, performance, recovery, or publication claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-transition-contract-llsca0
# Competency: asteroids-transition-contract.
# Case 3034: a keyboard-only community arcade night.
# Operating constraint: preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def evidence_asteroids_transition_contract_llsca0(states: int = 6, valid_transitions: int = 9, invalid_transitions: int = 0, pause_freezes_simulation: bool = True, restart_resets_run: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_llsca0 = "llsca0-0acsll-3034"
    assert evidence_variant_llsca0
    evidence_axes_llsca0 = (
        "playerllsca0",
        "seed3034",
        "input0acsll",
        "framellsca03034",
        "state3034llsca0",
        "playtest0acsll3034",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_llsca0)) == 6
    if states < 4 or valid_transitions < states:
        return False, states, "state-graph"
    if invalid_transitions > 0:
        return False, invalid_transitions, "illegal-transition"
    if not pause_freezes_simulation:
        return False, valid_transitions, "pause-leak"
    if not restart_resets_run:
        return False, valid_transitions, "restart-leak"
    assert valid_transitions >= states
    return True, valid_transitions, "explicit-scenes"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Transition Contract model · Theory Transition Contract
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Transition Contract: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 6887 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to model menu, playing, paused, life-lost, game-over, and restart transitions without hidden scene state; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Define trigger, guard, exit work, entry work, resulting state, and rejected transitions.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Transition Contract model · Theory Transition Contract.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Transition Contract model · Theory Transition Contract, keep Transition Contract connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define trigger, guard, exit work, entry work, resulting state, and rejected transitions.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Transition Contract model · Theory Transition Contract, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Transition Contract precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Transition Contract model · Theory Transition Contract, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 6887 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to model menu, playing, paused, life-lost, game-over, and restart transitions without hidden scene state; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Define trigger, guard, exit work, entry work, resulting state, and rejected transitions.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Transition Contract model · Theory Transition Contract, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: A scene can mutate directly into any other scene without a transition contract.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: asteroids-transition-contract-llsca0(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_asteroids_transition_contract_llsca0\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?states < 4)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?invalid_transitions > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not pause_freezes_simulation)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not restart_resets_run)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
